item.title: 21 April 2026 China+ Monitor China rates steady on NIM pressure; ample liquidity cushioning growth
item.description:
• China’s LPRs and de-facto policy rate were unchanged in April, amid pressure on banks’ margins.
  • Banks started a new round of deposit-rate cuts, given the liquidity glut in the system from weak loan demand.
  • The MoF is offering ultra-long special bonds at record levels, taking advantage of the risk-averse mood.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China’s LPRs and de-facto policy rate were unchanged in April, amid pressure on banks’ margins.
  • Banks started a new round of deposit-rate cuts, given the liquidity glut in the system from weak loan demand.
  • The MoF is offering ultra-long special bonds at record levels, taking advantage of the risk-averse mood.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 20 April 2026 China+ Monitor China speeding up fiscal support, to provide policy flexibility in H2
item.description:
• China is expediting fiscal measures to support investment by the end of H1...
  • ...Providing flexibility for additional support in H2 if the Iran war drags on and hurts global growth.
  • The residential property market is enjoying a ‘little spring’ with rising sale s, but a real recovery is still far off.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China is expediting fiscal measures to support investment by the end of H1...
  • ...Providing flexibility for additional support in H2 if the Iran war drags on and hurts global growth.
  • The residential property market is enjoying a ‘little spring’ with rising sale s, but a real recovery is still far off.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: CHINA+ DATA WRAP 16 April 2026: Q1 GDP growth bump likely the high-water mark
item.description: In one line: China's lopsided Q1 GDP growth bump likely the year's high-water mark
item.content:encoded: In one line: China's lopsided Q1 GDP growth bump likely the year's high-water mark

item.title: 17 April 2026 China+ Monitor All that glitters is not gold: China's flawed Q1 GDP print
item.description:
• China’s GDP growth rose to 5.0% in Q1, but it was highly dependent on robust exports...
  • ...Which are likely to slow as the oil price shock hits global growth.
  • Real household spending slowed and underlying consumption activity remains sluggish.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China’s GDP growth rose to 5.0% in Q1, but it was highly dependent on robust exports...
  • ...Which are likely to slow as the oil price shock hits global growth.
  • Real household spending slowed and underlying consumption activity remains sluggish.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 16 April 2026 China+ Monitor China's trade surplus squeezed by tech-led import surge
item.description:
• China’s trade surplus narrowed sharply in March, as import strength outpaced exports, hit by payback.
  • The import surge was led by high-tech items, with price effects outweighing geopolitical energy dynamics.
  • Exports were distorted by LNY effects, but underlying momentum was notably weaker for the Global South.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China’s trade surplus narrowed sharply in March, as import strength outpaced exports, hit by payback.
  • The import surge was led by high-tech items, with price effects outweighing geopolitical energy dynamics.
  • Exports were distorted by LNY effects, but underlying momentum was notably weaker for the Global South.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: PM Datanote: Money & Credit, China, March
item.description: In one line: Private sector credit showing early but uneven improvement
item.content:encoded: In one line: Private sector credit showing early but uneven improvement

item.title: 14 April 2026 China+ Monitor China's credit data indicates areas of stronger activity
item.description:
• China’s March credit data, albeit soft overall, points to a tentative private credit revival in select areas.
  • Rising pre-existing home sales likely drove mortgage demand; bottoming out is happening albeit slowly.
  • Policy-driven infrastructure investment probably supported improving underlying corporate credit.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China’s March credit data, albeit soft overall, points to a tentative private credit revival in select areas.
  • Rising pre-existing home sales likely drove mortgage demand; bottoming out is happening albeit slowly.
  • Policy-driven infrastructure investment probably supported improving underlying corporate credit.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 13 April 2026 China+ Monitor China's producer price rise largely due to energy-price shock
item.description:
• China’s first rise in producer prices in 41 months was partly due to the global energy-price shock...
  • ...But it does not indicate an improvement in demand fundamentals nor the exit from low inflation.
  • Producer prices for consumer goods continue to fall, while core consumer inflation is subdued.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China’s first rise in producer prices in 41 months was partly due to the global energy-price shock...
  • ...But it does not indicate an improvement in demand fundamentals nor the exit from low inflation.
  • Producer prices for consumer goods continue to fall, while core consumer inflation is subdued.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 9 April 2026 China+ Monitor Iran war hits China via trade channel, but with limited impact
item.description:
• China has ramped up energy production from alternative sources in the wake of the Iran war.
  • China has seen limited trade spillover; East Asian PMIs show a common theme of higher oil-driven input costs.
  • Hong Kong’s PMI plunged on war uncertainty, with price pressures yet to feed through. 
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China has ramped up energy production from alternative sources in the wake of the Iran war.
  • China has seen limited trade spillover; East Asian PMIs show a common theme of higher oil-driven input costs.
  • Hong Kong’s PMI plunged on war uncertainty, with price pressures yet to feed through. 
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: PM Datanote: Foreign Reserves, China, March
item.description: In one line: China’s big drop mainly due to global market volatility, rather than domestic economy
item.content:encoded: In one line: China’s big drop mainly due to global market volatility, rather than domestic economy

item.title: 7 April 2026 China+ Monitor China's profit turnaround meshes with strategic drive for high-tech
item.description:
• China’s 18.9% jump in manufacturing profits in January-to-February was largely due to high-tech sectors.
  • Profit growth is likely to be hit by higher oil prices, but the damage should be less severe than in 2022.
  • Auto sales should improve from their poor start to the year, but brutal competition is squeezing profits.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China’s 18.9% jump in manufacturing profits in January-to-February was largely due to high-tech sectors.
  • Profit growth is likely to be hit by higher oil prices, but the damage should be less severe than in 2022.
  • Auto sales should improve from their poor start to the year, but brutal competition is squeezing profits.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: PM Datanote: NBS Manufacturing PMI, China, March
item.description: In one line:  China’s manufacturing PMI buoyed by post-holiday seasonality; oil price shock hits input prices
item.content:encoded: In one line:  China’s manufacturing PMI buoyed by post-holiday seasonality; oil price shock hits input prices

item.title: CHINA+ DATA WRAP 31 March 2026: China's manufacturing activity resilient
item.description: In one line: China's manufacturing activity resilient, despite jump in energy-related costs
item.content:encoded: In one line: China's manufacturing activity resilient, despite jump in energy-related costs

item.title: 1 April 2026 China+ Monitor China shrugs off initial impact of oil-price storm
item.description:
• The official March PMIs support our view that China will be relatively resilient to the energy-price shock.
  • Output and demand activity indicators were solid, despite the surging manufacturing input price gauge.
  • Private-sector sentiment took a small dent in March, but nothing like the fall amid last year’s tariff war.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• The official March PMIs support our view that China will be relatively resilient to the energy-price shock.
  • Output and demand activity indicators were solid, despite the surging manufacturing input price gauge.
  • Private-sector sentiment took a small dent in March, but nothing like the fall amid last year’s tariff war.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 30 March 2026 China+ Monitor China's elderly-care insurance reform will lift GDP, but only slowly
item.description:
• China’s national long-term care reform should boost GDP by almost 1% by 2030...
  • ...But more is needed to replace the 6%-of-GDP decline in housing investment since 2021.
  • The BoJ’s new natural interest rate and CPI estimate don’t change the big picture; oil prices are key.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China’s national long-term care reform should boost GDP by almost 1% by 2030...
  • ...But more is needed to replace the 6%-of-GDP decline in housing investment since 2021.
  • The BoJ’s new natural interest rate and CPI estimate don’t change the big picture; oil prices are key.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: March 2026 - China+ Chartbook
item.description:
CHINA+ OUTLOOK
- CHINA RELATIVELY INSULATED FROM OIL-PRICE SPIKE
- BOJ WOULD HIKE EARLY IN A $150/BL OIL SCENARIO
- KOREA'S CHIP EXPORT RISE OFFSETS OIL IMPORT BILL
item.content:encoded:
CHINA+ OUTLOOK
- CHINA RELATIVELY INSULATED FROM OIL-PRICE SPIKE
- BOJ WOULD HIKE EARLY IN A $150/BL OIL SCENARIO
- KOREA'S CHIP EXPORT RISE OFFSETS OIL IMPORT BILL

item.title: CHINA+ DATA WRAP 24 March 2026: Japan's inflation fell, ahead of oil price surge
item.description: In one line: Japan's inflation fell in February, ahead of oil price surge; Services firms' sentiment dives to mid-2020 level
item.content:encoded: In one line: Japan's inflation fell in February, ahead of oil price surge; Services firms' sentiment dives to mid-2020 level

item.title: 25 March 2026 China+ Monitor BoJ signalling readiness to respond to prolonged oil-price shock
item.description:
• Governor Ueda said yesterday he expects a moderate rise in underlying inflation...
  • ...The BoJ’s base case appears to be a swift oil-price fall, with little effect on the long-term inflation outlook.
  • But persistently sky-high energy prices would drive up food prices and could force an April rate hike.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• Governor Ueda said yesterday he expects a moderate rise in underlying inflation...
  • ...The BoJ’s base case appears to be a swift oil-price fall, with little effect on the long-term inflation outlook.
  • But persistently sky-high energy prices would drive up food prices and could force an April rate hike.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 24 March 2026 China+ Monitor China's residential market still grinding through high inventories
item.description:
• China residential property market remains in the doldrums, with a 43% drop in sales month-to-date…
  • …Construction area is still declining, while developer funding improved slightly thanks to policy support.
  • Korea’s 20-day exports maintained robust growth in March, riding strong semiconductor demand.
Delivered by PolitePaul service
item.content:encoded:
• China residential property market remains in the doldrums, with a 43% drop in sales month-to-date…
  • …Construction area is still declining, while developer funding improved slightly thanks to policy support.
  • Korea’s 20-day exports maintained robust growth in March, riding strong semiconductor demand.
Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: CHINA+ DATA WRAP 23 March 2026: Korean exports jump, thanks to chip sales
item.description: In one line: Korean exports jump, thanks to chip sales; oil product shipments surge
item.content:encoded: In one line: Korean exports jump, thanks to chip sales; oil product shipments surge
